era: 1991. Grunge. E=10 -- THE CLOSING ARC BEGINS. Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington. Epic Records. Ten. The debut. 13x platinum. The album that documented a generation's emotional landscape in eleven tracks.
associated_musicians: Chris Cornell (Temple of the Dog, Hunger Strike duet with Vedder)
Andrew Wood (Mother Love Bone, catalyst for Pearl Jam's formation)
key_albums: Ten (1991, Epic, Parashar) -- E=10. The debut. #2 Billboard 200. 13x platinum RIAA. Alive, Even Flow, Jeremy, Black. 53 minutes, 11 tracks.
Vs. (1993, Epic) -- follow-up to Ten. Shipped 950,000 copies first week, a record. More aggressive, less polished.
Vitalogy (1994, Epic) -- third album. Released on vinyl weeks before CD. Experimental expansion.
No Code (1996, Epic) -- fourth album. Radical sonic shift. Polaroid imagery. Commercial decline begins.

theory_nodes.concept: Ten (1991, Epic, Rick Parashar): released August 27, 1991, London Bridge Studio, Seattle. Reached #2 Billboard 200. 13x platinum RIAA. 53 minutes, 11 tracks. 'Once' opens -- heavy, unconventional. 'Alive' (biographical: Vedder discovers stepfather is not biological father, biological father died before meeting). 'Even Flow' -- Gossard descending riff, homeless man lyric. 'Jeremy' -- based on January 8, 1991 Richardson TX event; MTV VMA Video of the Year 1993. 'Black' -- not released as commercial single, 6:22 ballad. The album that documented a generation's emotional landscape.
theory_nodes.description: Ten (1991) -- the debut, the 13x platinum artifact, the closing arc's opening entry.

theory_nodes.concept: The closing arc (degrees 349-353): Pearl Jam Ten (349, E=10), TAD peak (350, E=10), The Accused peak (351, E=10), Botch WATR (352, E=10), TAAS peak (353, E=9). The engine's final five degrees. Not a descent from the summit -- a completion arc documenting the PNW music tradition's maximum expressions beyond the grunge core. Opens at maximum commercial scale (13x platinum), moves toward the most technically extreme underground entries.
theory_nodes.description: The closing arc structure -- five degrees, 4x E=10, 1x E=9, from commercial maximum to underground extreme.

theory_nodes.concept: The Vedder-Gossard-McCready triangle: Gossard (primary compositional architect, rhythm guitar, riff-as-architecture: Once, Even Flow), McCready (blues-influenced expressive lead guitar, catharsis over virtuosity: Alive solo), Vedder (baritone, sustained intensity, emotional directness without mediation, compression-release at phrase level). The specific combination is not the sum of parts -- it is the configuration that produced Ten and nothing else would have.
theory_nodes.description: The core triangle -- Gossard's architecture, McCready's expression, Vedder's voice.

theory_nodes.concept: Temple of the Dog (1991, A&M Records): Andrew Wood tribute organized by Chris Cornell. Gossard, Ament, McCready participate. Vedder appears on two tracks including 'Hunger Strike' (Cornell/Vedder duet). Released April 1991, four months before Ten (August 1991). First wide exposure to Vedder's voice. The formation context: Mother Love Bone's Andrew Wood died March 19, 1990; the members who would become Pearl Jam formed around the grief and its productive energy.
theory_nodes.description: Temple of the Dog -- the formation context and first wide Vedder exposure.

theory_nodes.concept: Rick Parashar production: London Bridge Studio, Seattle. Organic, room-filling, guitar-forward. Vedder's voice not processed to smoothness -- roughness and grain preserved. The specific sonic environment that made Ten's emotional content accessible without diluting it. Distinguishes Ten from more polished grunge records: sounds like people playing instruments in a room, because it was.
theory_nodes.description: Parashar's production -- preserved roughness, organic recording, the specific Ten sound.

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill, Seattle: recurring engine geography. Pearl Jam formed and rehearsed here. Same neighborhood that produced Soundgarden (346), Alice in Chains (347 partial). The center that receives and concentrates the periphery's maximum expressions into commercially accessible forms. The closing arc opens from the same geography as the summit.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill -- the engine's recurring center, Pearl Jam formation geography.

cross_references.relationship: Ten and Nevermind both released in 1991. Nevermind = E=10 through detonation (cultural maximum, Billboard #1). Ten = E=10 through emotional precision and sustained commercial viability. Both are grunge, both are 1991, both are Capitol Hill / Aberdeen formation. The summit completes at 348; the closing arc opens at 349.

Both Capitol Hill. Both E=10. Soundgarden = completeness (70 minutes, full alt metal range). Ten = emotional precision and debut scale. The closing arc's opening entry references the summit's second E=10.

Prior Pearl Jam entry at E=9. Degree 349 re-enters at E=10 specifically for Ten as album entry. The full Pearl Jam arc across two engine positions: catalog (341, E=9) and debut album specific (349, E=10).

E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Closing arc's first E=20. Both entries about maximum completeness -- all emotional registers simultaneously (Ten) paired with all acoustic layers simultaneously (peak fall dawn). The most complete seasonal convergence paired with the most emotionally complete debut album in the PNW catalog.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Closing arc's first E=20. Ten (11 tracks, all emotional registers) paired with all-layers peak fall dawn convergence (elk rut + active storm + full dawn bird chorus simultaneously). Both about maximum completeness across multiple simultaneous registers. Both Pacific Northwest. The closing arc opens with E=20.

closing_arc: 349 Pearl Jam Ten E=10 → 350 TAD Peak E=10 → 351 Accused Peak E=10 → 352 Botch WATR E=10 → 353 TAAS Peak E=9. THE ENGINE'S FINAL FIVE.
